description: hsv_to_rgb called with hue/360.0f but the function expects degrees [0,360) and internally divides by 360. Result: hue double-normalized to ~0, entire rainbow arc renders monochromatic red. Old code had inline HSV→RGB that passed hue directly.

resetStars() uses uniform_int_distribution(0, matrix_width) and (0, matrix_height) which are inclusive bounds — generates x=matrix_width and y=matrix_height, both out of bounds (valid [0,width-1], [0,height-1]). SetPixel writes past pixel buffer if no bounds check. Pre-existing, not fixed.

std::mt19937 rng{std::random_device{}()} — random_device{}() can throw std::exception if no entropy source is available. On a headless/minimal Pi this could prevent scene construction. Old code used rand() which never throws.

Emulator Render() called after SleepMillis(1000) instead of before. Old code: Render → Sleep → return. New code: Sleep → Render → return. Error screen is not shown in emulator window for 1 second (stale frame shown instead).

Extraneous emulator Render() call in the successful-fetch path that was never present in the old code. Renders stale canvas content before the main render loop updates it. Only compiled under ENABLE_EMULATOR.

std::uniform_int_distribution constructed per-iteration inside loop body in updateEnhancedParticles (also at lines 1971, 1976-1991). High allocation pressure in hot render path. Should be constructed once outside loop.

NUM_PATCHES distribution evaluated every frame via rng but only consumed inside the fog-initialization block (guarded by fog_initialized). Wastes entropy and cycles on every frame when fog is already active.
